onboarding.question:
1. Define what "ambient awareness only" means for the logged-out indicator — should it be static text-only, non-interactive, and appear in a fixed location without any modal, popover, or tooltip affordances?
2. Specify the exact retry behavior on authentication failure — should users see a single retry button, have automatic retry on timeout, or see retry disabled after a second failure attempt?
onboarding.designContext:
## Approved Product Spec
# Onboarding — Product Spec

## Problem
New and returning users need a frictionless path from first landing to the main chat home screen. Unauthenticated users risk losing their conversation history without realizing it, which undermines trust and conversion. The onboarding experience must get users to the home screen quickly, make the value of signing in clear without forcing it, and ensure no one is surprised by data loss.

## Target Users
- **New users** — arriving for the first time, no existing account
- **Returning users** — have an existing account, signing back in
- **Logged-out new users** — new users who choose to explore before signing up

## User Stories
1. As a new user, I want to sign up quickly so that I can start using Health360 without unnecessary friction.
2. As a returning user, I want to sign in and land directly on the home screen so that I can pick up where I left off.
3. As a logged-out user, I want to explore the product before committing to sign up so that I can understand its value first.
4. As a logged-out user, I want a subtle, persistent indicator that I am not signed in so that I am never surprised by the implications of being logged out.
5. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned before my session expires so that I can sign up and save my conversation before it's lost.
6. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned when I try to navigate away or close the tab so that I don't accidentally lose my conversation.
7. As a logged-out user, I want to know that signing in will preserve my conversation so that I have a clear reason to create an account at that moment.
8. As a new user completing sign-up from a logged-out session, I want my existing conversation to carry over so that I don't lose the context I've already built.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Unauthenticated users can access the chat interface and interact with the AI without signing in.
2. A persistent, non-dismissible indicator is visible to logged-out users in the top-right area of the screen, clearly stating they are not signed in.
3. The indicator does not block interaction or prompt sign-up — it is ambient awareness only.
4. The logged-out indicator remains visible at all times while the user is not authenticated.
5. New users can sign up via SSO (Google, Apple Sign-In) only — no password-based registration.
6. Returning users can sign in via SSO and are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
7. New users who complete sign-up are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
8. When a logged-out user's session is approaching expiry (at 10 minutes remaining) AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a warning is surfaced informing them their conversation will be lost if they do not sign in.
9. When a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a custom in-app modal is surfaced informing them their conversation will be lost.
10. After the first complete AI response in a session, an in-conversation nudge surfaces informing the logged-out user that their conversation will not be saved unless they sign in, rendered as a conversational system message with a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action.
11. The in-conversation nudge fires exactly once per session and does not repeat on subsequent exchanges.
12. Both warnings and the in-conversation nudge include a clear call-to-action to sign up or sign in.
13. Upon completing sign-up or sign-in from any warning CTA, the user's conversation is fully preserved and accessible in their conversation history.
14. If the logged-out user has no conversation history, no session expiry or navigation warning is shown.
15. If the user dismisses a warning and does not sign in, the session expires and the conversation is lost — no further prompts.
16. There is no forced sign-up wall at any point in the logged-out experience.
17. The path from landing page to authenticated home screen requires a maximum of 3 user interactions: (1) landing on the logged-out chat interface, (2) tapping a sign-in or sign-up call-to-action, (3) completing SSO and being taken directly to the authenticated home screen. No intermediate profile setup, goal-setting, or confirmation screens are permitted.
18. Goal setting does not occur during onboarding — it happens post-onboarding through the AI conversation.
19. On initial page load, the UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately while authentication state resolution occurs in the background.
20. If authentication state resolution succeeds within 2 seconds of initial load, the user transitions to authenticated state without visual disruption and conversation history loads.
21. If authentication state resolution fails or exceeds 2 seconds, the user remains in logged-out mode and a retry affordance appears only upon confirmed auth failure.

## Edge Cases
- User signs up from the warning CTA but the session expires before authentication completes — conversation cannot be recovered; surface a clear, friendly message.
- User begins sign-up flow from the warning but abandons it — session continues to tick down; conversation is lost on expiry with no additional prompt.
- User dismisses the navigate-away modal but remains on the page — modal does not re-trigger unless they attempt to navigate away again.
- User has multiple tabs open with separate logged-out conversations — each tab is treated independently.
- Returning user attempts to sign in but SSO provider is unavailable — surface a clear error; do not block the logged-out experience.
- New user completes SSO but the account creation step fails — handle gracefully; do not drop the user into an inconsistent state.
- Session expiry warning fires at 10 minutes remaining — sufficient lead time to complete SSO sign-up before expiry.
- The in-conversation nudge fires once per session — it does not repeat on every exchange.
- Authentication state resolution fails on initial page load — user remains in logged-out mode and may attempt retry; retry attempt should not loop indefinitely or create cascading failures.
- Authentication state resolution exceeds 2 seconds but succeeds before user initiates action — user receives transparent transition to authenticated state without losing any interaction context.

## Non-Goals
- Goal setting, profile building, and AI coaching — these begin after the user reaches the home screen.
- Password-based authentication — SSO only.
- Forced sign-up walls or conversion prompts outside of the defined warning triggers and in-conversation nudge.
- Auto-saving or server-side backup of logged-out conversations without explicit user action.
- Changing session expiry duration — infrastructure concern outside this spec.
- Social or referral flows — not part of the initial vision.

## Design Direction
**Dark mode primary — Archon Labs aesthetic (getarchon.dev).** Visual language: minimal, high negative space. The interface recedes; the conversation leads. Chrome is nearly invisible until needed.

**Color palette (extracted from getarchon.dev):**
- Background: `#0A0E27` (dark navy, almost black)
- Surfaces: `#151B38` (fractionally lighter, subtle elevation)
- Text primary: `#F5F5F5` (off-white, full contrast on dark background)
- Text muted: `rgba(245, 245, 245, 0.6)` (neutral grey for secondary labels)
- Accent gradient: 135° from `#8B7FE8` (soft violet) to `#4FADA8` (muted teal)

**Signature treatment: Pulsing glow effect**
- Radial gradient: violet-teal (same palette as accent gradient)
- Animation: async heartbeat pulse (not synchronized to user input or other glow instances)
- Opacity cycle: 25% → 35% → 25% over 2.5 seconds, ease-in-out cubic bezier (cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1))
- Position: fixed behind UI elements (prompt bar and Auth Sheet buttons)
- Applied in exactly two places: (1) chat home background behind prompt bar, (2) Auth Sheet behind SSO buttons
- Behavior as conversation fills: blur radius shrinks (200px → 100px), opacity reduces (25–35% → 12–16%) over ~4 messages or 60% viewport fill
- Minimum state: 12–16% opacity, ~100px blur radius — still present, clearly visible
- Each glow instance pulses independently — no synchronization between home glow and Auth Sheet glow
- Nowhere else in the product
- Conveys: ambient warmth, alive-ness without distraction

## Open Questions
- [type: engineering] [blocking: no] Unauthenticated session TTL is provisionally set at 60 minutes inactivity-based expiry with a warning at 10 minutes remaining — needs infrastructure confirmation before engineering begins.

## Current Design Draft
# Onboarding — Design Spec

## Figma
TBD

## Design Direction

**Dark mode primary — Archon Labs aesthetic (getarchon.dev).** Visual language: minimal, high negative space. The interface recedes; the conversation leads. Chrome is nearly invisible until needed.

**Archon Labs Visual Language:**
- Dark navy/charcoal background (#0A0A0F) with off-white text (#F8F8F7) — high contrast, clean
- Accent colors (violet #7C6FCD, teal #4FAFA8) used intentionally on interactive elements, labels, and highlights — never decorative
- Gradient text (135° violet → teal) on primary headings and UI chrome only — hand-authored UI elements like wordmark, prompt placeholder, CTA labels, section headers — never in conversational messages or AI responses
- Typography hierarchy: system-ui, weights 400/500/600, no custom fonts
- Signature treatment: dual independent pulsing glow effect. Violet and teal glows pulse independently (55–100% opacity cycle, 4s duration, ease-in-out cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1), offset -1.8s on teal, 80px blur radius). Glow shrinks and fades as conversation fills viewport: blur radius 80px → 80px (maintained), opacity 55–100% → 55–100% (maintained throughout session). Each glow instance is independent; if home and Auth Sheet glows are visible simultaneously, they do not synchronize to each other.
- Minimal chrome — buttons, cards, and surfaces are understated until interaction
- Extreme negative space — 60–70% empty on hero screens, 30–40% empty within containers

**Color Application Rules:**
- Primary text (#F8F8F7): body copy, conversation content, labels
- Muted text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)): secondary labels, timestamps, disabled states
- Subtle text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.18)): placeholders, tertiary information
- Accent (violet #7C6FCD): focus states, active tabs, primary CTAs, badges
- Accent (teal #4FAFA8): secondary CTAs, hover states, accent badges
- Gradient (violet → teal): primary headings, wordmark, prompt placeholder, UI chrome only — never in conversational content
- Border (rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07)): dividers, card edges, subtle elevation

**Interactive Element Styling:**
- Rounded pill containers: 40–48px border-radius, 1px teal borders at 15% opacity (#4FAFA8)
- Glow effect: faint radial gradient halo positioned behind rounded pills and interactive elements, conveying ambient warmth without distraction
- Glow opacity cycle: 55–100%, maintained throughout conversation
- Glow animation: 4s pulse cycle, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) easing. Violet and teal pulsing independently, offset -1.8s on teal. Each instance independent — no cross-instance synchronization.
- Starter chips fade choreography: 150ms parallel fade-out on first AI message, ease-out easing

## Brand

**Glow (Signature Effect)**

Two independent radial glows — violet and teal — rendered separately and animated asynchronously.

**Violet glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.42) 0%, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.14) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-violet {
  0%   { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 1.00; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.90; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.58; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-violet 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
```

**Teal glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.34) 0%, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.11) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-teal {
  0%   { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 0.95; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.85; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-teal 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
animation-delay: -1.8s;   /* offset so violet and teal pulse out of sync */
```

**Usage rules:**
- Applied in exactly two places: (1) chat home background behind prompt bar, (2) Auth Sheet behind SSO buttons
- Positioned as a faint radial halo behind rounded pill containers and interactive elements
- Nowhere else in the product
- Each instance is independent — home glow and Auth Sheet glow never synchronize, even if both visible simultaneously
- Both glows are decorative only (aria-hidden="true") and carry no semantic meaning
- Opacity cycle: 50–100% over 4s duration, ease-in-out cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Blur radius: 80px
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`: static opacity at 75% (middle of cycle), no pulsing, 80px blur maintained

## Nav Shell
- Top bar: Health360 wordmark left (text, not avatar), account indicator right (44×44pt minimum)
- Logged-out state: muted ghost chip labeled "Sign in" in top-right nav position
- No bottom navigation — single-surface chat experience
- Prompt bar pinned to bottom of viewport — always bottom-anchored
- Starter chips in horizontal scrollable row between top bar and prompt bar

## Screens

### Screen 1: Chat Home (Logged-Out Default)

**Purpose:** The primary conversational interface. Logged-out users land here and can interact with the AI without signing in. Serves user stories US-3, US-4, US-5.

**States:** default | loading | conversation-active | empty | error

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Top Navigation Bar (Fixed, always visible)**
- Background: `--bg` (#0A0A0F)
- Height: 64px on mobile, 56px on web
- Padding: 16px horizontal, 12px vertical
- Layout: flex, space-between

**Left Section (Wordmark only):**
- Health360 wordmark: gradient text (135° violet #7C6FCD → teal #4FAFA8), system-ui 20px 600 weight
- No tagline in top nav

**Right Section (Account Indicator):**
- Logged-out state: text label "Not signed in", 12px system-ui 500 weight, text color rgba(245, 245, 245, 0.6), always visible, non-dismissible
- Logged-in state: user's first name (or email if first name unavailable), 12px system-ui 500 weight, text color #F8F8F7, clickable to open account menu with sign-out option

**Conversation Area (Scrollable, centered)**
- Max-width: 680px
- Full-height scrollable container positioned below top bar
- Padding: 32px horizontal (mobile reduces to 16px on very small screens)
- Empty state on load displays: centered "Health360" heading (gradient text, 28px, 600 weight) with tagline "All your health. One conversation." directly below (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 12px 400 weight, 4px gap between heading and tagline)

**Starter Chips Row (Positioned between conversation area and prompt bar)**
- Horizontal scrollable row, 16px gap between chips
- Chips displayed as rounded pills (40–48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), dark surface background (#13131A)
- Chips fade out on first AI message: 150ms parallel fade-out, ease-out easing
- Removed from DOM after fade completes (or hidden with display: none)

**Prompt Bar (Pinned to bottom of viewport)**
- Always accessible, never scrolls out of view
- Rounded pill container (40–48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), dark surface background (#13131A)
- Input field inside: placeholder text "Ask anything about your health" in subtle text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.18))
- Glow effect positioned behind prompt bar: dual radial glows (violet + teal) pulsing asynchronously, 80px blur radius, 50–100% opacity cycle, 4s duration, offset -1.8s on teal

### Screen 1.1: Chat Home (Logged-In)

**Purpose:** Logged-in variant of the chat home. User's first name replaces "Not signed in" indicator; account menu accessible on click.

**States:** default | loading | conversation-active | empty | error

**Top Navigation Bar (Fixed, always visible)**
- Background: `--bg` (#0A0A0F)
- Height: 64px on mobile, 56px on web
- Padding: 16px horizontal, 12px vertical
- Layout: flex, space-between

**Left Section (Wordmark):**
- Health360 wordmark: gradient text (135° violet #7C6FCD → teal #4FAFA8), system-ui 20px 600 weight

**Right Section (Account Indicator):**
- User's first name (or email if first name unavailable), 12px system-ui 500 weight, text color #F8F8F7
- Clickable text to trigger account menu (44×44pt minimum touch target, no visible button chrome)
- Account menu opens as a small dropdown positioned top-right: "Sign out" button, background `--surface` (#13131A), border 1px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07), rounded corners 12px, padding 8px vertical

**Conversation Area, Starter Chips Row, Prompt Bar:** Identical to logged-out variant

### Screen 1.2: Chat Home — Loading State

**Purpose:** Displayed while conversation response is being streamed or AI is generating.

**Visual Treatment:**
- Conversation area shows existing messages (if any)
- Below the last user message, a loading indicator appears: spinner (12px diameter, rotating continuously at 1s per rotation, primary text color #F8F8F7) with adjacent text "Thinking..." (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 12px 400 weight)
- Prompt bar remains active and accepts new input (non-blocking load)

### Screen 1.3: Chat Home — Empty State

**Purpose:** Displayed on first load before any user input.

**Visual Treatment:**
- Centered "Health360" heading (gradient text, 28px, 600 weight)
- Tagline below: "All your health. One conversation." (muted text rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45), system-ui 12px 400 weight)
- Starter chips row visible below tagline
- Prompt bar ready for input

### Screen 1.4: Chat Home — Error State

**Purpose:** Displayed when AI response fails or a critical error occurs.

**Visual Treatment:**
- Existing conversation preserved
- Error message banner displays above the prompt bar: "Something went wrong. Please try again." (error text #e06c75, system-ui 14px 400 weight), background rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.1), border 1px #e06c75, rounded corners 12px, padding 12px
- Prompt bar remains active; user can retry
- Banner dismissible via X button (24×24pt, transparent background, error text on hover)

### Screen 2: Auth Sheet

**Purpose:** SSO authentication interface. Opened from "Sign in" text label or warning modal CTAs. Serves user stories US-1, US-2, US-6.

**States:** default | loading | error

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Mobile:**
- Bottom sheet, slides up from bottom over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Full viewport height or content-driven height (whichever fits)
- Rounded top corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical (top and bottom)

**Web:**
- Centered modal overlay
- Fades in + scales up (0.95 → 1.0) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Max-width: 400px
- Rounded corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Scrim (darkened background): rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), clickable to dismiss
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Header Section:**
- Close button: "X" icon, top-left corner (mobile) or top-right corner (web), 44×44pt minimum, transparent background, muted text (#F8F8F7 at 45% opacity) on hover/focus
- Heading: "Welcome back.", gradient text (135° violet #7C6FCD → teal #4FAFA8), system-ui 20px 600 weight, centered
- Subheading below heading: "Your conversation will be saved when you sign in.", muted text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)), system-ui 14px 400 weight, centered, 12px gap below heading

**SSO Buttons Section:**
- Two buttons stacked vertically, 16px gap between them
- Each button: rounded pill (40–48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), `--surface` background (#13131A), 56px height minimum, full width
- Button text: "Sign in with Google" and "Sign in with Apple", system-ui 16px 500 weight, primary text (#F8F8F7)
- Glow effect: violet-teal dual radial glows pulsing independently behind buttons, 50–100% opacity cycle, 4s duration, offset -1.8s on teal, 80px blur radius
- Glow positioned absolutely behind button container, not clipped
- On hover (web only): border-color increases to teal 30% opacity, background increases to rgba(19, 19, 26, 1)
- On focus: same styling as hover, outline visible
- On active/press (mobile): background darkens, button remains tappable

**Loading State:**
- Triggered on SSO provider redirect
- Button text changes to "Signing in with [Provider]..."
- Spinner appears left of text inside button (12px diameter, rotating at 1s per rotation over 0.15s ease, primary text color)
- Button becomes disabled (opacity 0.6, no pointer events)
- Sheet remains open, not dismissible during loading

**Error State:**
- Triggered if SSO provider is unavailable or authentication fails
- Error message displays above buttons: "Sign in failed. Please try again.", error text (#e06c75), system-ui 14px 400 weight, centered, 16px margin below subheading
- Buttons re-enable and are tappable again
- Close button remains functional — user can dismiss and try again later

**Dismiss Behavior:**
- Escape key closes sheet
- Scrim tap (web only) closes sheet
- Close button ("X") closes sheet
- On close: sheet slides down (mobile) or fades out + scales down (web) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Focus returns to trigger element (the "Sign in" text label or CTA button that opened the sheet)
- Sheet does not dismiss during loading state

**Accessibility:**
- Sheet role: dialog, aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby="auth-sheet-heading"
- Heading element: h1 with id="auth-sheet-heading"
- Close button: aria-label="Close", visible focus state
- Focus trap: Tab cycles through close button → subheading → Google button → Apple button → close button (loop)
- Escape key triggers close
- Screen reader announces: "Dialog: Welcome back. Your conversation will be saved when you sign in."
- SSO buttons read as: "Button: Sign in with Google", "Button: Sign in with Apple"
- Error message is announced on state change

**Interactions & Animations:**
- Entry: 0.35s slide-up (mobile) or fade-in + scale (web)
- Exit: 0.35s slide-down (mobile) or fade-out + scale (web)
- Button hover: 0.20s border and background transition
- Loading spinner: continuous rotation, respects motion preference
- Glow: independent violet-teal pulse, 50–100% opacity over 4s, offset -1.8s on teal

**Notes:**
- The glow effect is positioned as a fixed or absolute background layer behind the SSO button container — it extends beyond button edges
- Glow is decorative only (aria-hidden="true")
- No shadow or elevation effect on buttons — only the glow provides depth
- On success (after SSO completes), the sheet dismisses automatically and the user is taken to Chat Home (logged-in state) with conversation preserved
- Session expiry or auth failure during SSO is handled gracefully: user sees error message, buttons re-enable, user can retry or close

### Screen 3: Session Expiry Warning Modal

**Purpose:** Displayed when a logged-out user's session is 10 minutes from expiry AND they have at least one conversation exchange. Serves user stories US-5, US-7.

**States:** default | loading

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Mobile:**
- Bottom sheet, slides up from bottom over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Rounded top corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Web:**
- Centered modal overlay
- Fades in + scales up (0.95 → 1.0) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Max-width: 400px
- Rounded corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Scrim (darkened background): rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), clickable to dismiss
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Header Section:**
- Close button: "X" icon, top-right corner, 44×44pt minimum, transparent background, muted text on hover/focus
- Heading: "Your conversation will expire in 10 minutes", primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 16px 600 weight, centered, no gradient
- Body copy below heading: "Sign up or sign in to save it.", muted text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)), system-ui 14px 400 weight, centered, 12px gap below heading

**CTA Buttons Section:**
- Two buttons stacked vertically, 16px gap between them
- First button (primary): "Sign up", rounded pill (40–48px border-radius), gradient background (135° violet #7C6FCD → teal #4FAFA8), primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 16px 500 weight, 56px height minimum, full width
- Second button (secondary): "Sign in", rounded pill (40–48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), transparent background, primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 16px 500 weight, 56px height minimum, full width
- On hover (web only): button backgrounds darken, border strengthens
- On focus: visible outline

**Dismiss Behavior:**
- Escape key closes modal
- Scrim tap (web only) closes modal
- Close button ("X") closes modal
- On close: modal slides down (mobile) or fades out + scales down (web) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Modal does not re-trigger after dismissal, even if the user continues chatting
- If user dismisses and does not sign in, session continues to tick down; conversation is lost on expiry with no additional prompt

**Loading State (if user taps Sign up or Sign in):**
- Selected button text changes to "Signing in..." or "Signing up..."
- Spinner appears left of text (12px diameter, rotating at 1s, primary text color)
- Button becomes disabled (opacity 0.6, no pointer events)
- Modal remains open
- On auth success: modal dismisses and user is taken to Chat Home with conversation preserved
- On auth error: error message displays above buttons, buttons re-enable

**Accessibility:**
- Modal role: dialog, aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby="expiry-warning-heading"
- Heading element: h2 with id="expiry-warning-heading"
- Close button: aria-label="Close"
- Focus trap: Tab cycles through close button → Sign up button → Sign in button → close button (loop)
- Escape key triggers close
- Screen reader announces: "Dialog: Your conversation will expire in 10 minutes. Sign up or sign in to save it."
- Buttons read as: "Button: Sign up", "Button: Sign in"

**Interactions & Animations:**
- Entry: 0.35s slide-up (mobile) or fade-in + scale (web)
- Exit: 0.35s slide-down (mobile) or fade-out + scale (web)
- Button hover: 0.20s background transition
- Loading spinner: continuous rotation, respects motion preference

### Screen 4: Navigate-Away Warning Modal

**Purpose:** Displayed when a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange. Serves user story US-6.

**States:** default | loading

**Layout (Mobile & Web):**

**Mobile:**
- Bottom sheet, slides up from bottom over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Rounded top corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Web:**
- Centered modal overlay
- Fades in + scales up (0.95 → 1.0) over 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Max-width: 400px
- Rounded corners: 16px border-radius
- Background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Scrim (darkened background): rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5), NOT clickable to dismiss (user must choose an action)
- Padding: 24px horizontal, 32px vertical

**Header Section (No close button):**
- Heading: "Your conversation will be lost", primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 16px 600 weight, centered, no gradient
- Body copy below heading: "If you leave, your conversation won't be saved unless you sign in first.", muted text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)), system-ui 14px 400 weight, centered, 12px gap below heading

**CTA Buttons Section:**
- Two buttons stacked vertically, 16px gap between them
- First button (primary): "Stay", rounded pill (40–48px border-radius), gradient background (135° violet #7C6FCD → teal #4FAFA8), primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 16px 500 weight, 56px height minimum, full width
- Second button (destructive secondary): "Leave anyway", rounded pill (40–48px border-radius), error border 1px #e06c75, transparent background, error text (#e06c75), system-ui 16px 500 weight, 56px height minimum, full width

**Button Behavior:**
- "Stay" button closes the modal and returns user to chat (no navigation away)
- "Leave anyway" button allows the navigation to proceed or tab to close
- No close button; user must choose an action to proceed
- No re-triggering: if user stays, modal does not appear again unless they attempt to navigate away again

**Accessibility:**
- Modal role: dialog, aria-modal="true", aria-labelledby="navigate-away-heading"
- Heading element: h2 with id="navigate-away-heading"
- Focus trap: Tab cycles through Stay button → Leave anyway button → Stay button (loop)
- Escape key does NOT close this modal (user must choose)
- Screen reader announces: "Dialog: Your conversation will be lost. If you leave, your conversation won't be saved unless you sign in first."
- Buttons read as: "Button: Stay", "Button: Leave anyway"

**Interactions & Animations:**
- Entry: 0.35s slide-up (mobile) or fade-in + scale (web)
- Exit on "Stay": 0.35s slide-down (mobile) or fade-out + scale (web), return to chat
- Exit on "Leave anyway": navigation proceeds as requested

### Screen 5: In-Conversation Sign-In Nudge

**Purpose:** Displayed after the first AI response in a logged-out session to inform the user that their conversation will not be saved. Serves user stories US-5, US-7.

**States:** default (inline in conversation thread)

**Visual Treatment:**
- Appears as an inline card (not a modal, banner, or overlay) directly below the first AI response in the conversation thread
- Card background: `--surface` (#13131A)
- Card border: 1px solid rgba(139, 127, 232, 0.3) (violet accent at 30% opacity)
- Card padding: 16px
- Card rounded corners: 12px
- Card width: 100% (full conversation width, respects max-width of conversation area)
- Margin: 12px top and bottom (spacing from surrounding messages)

**Content:**
- Heading: "Your conversation won't be saved", primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 14px 600 weight
- Body text below heading: "Sign in or sign up to keep your chat history for later.", muted text (rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)), system-ui 12px 400 weight, 8px gap below heading
- CTA button aligned right or full-width on mobile: "Sign in", rounded pill (40–48px border-radius), teal border 15% opacity (#4FAFA8), transparent background, primary text (#F8F8F7), system-ui 14px 500 weight, 44×44pt minimum

**Behavior:**
- Displayed once per session immediately after the first AI response completes
- Non-blocking: user can continue chatting without dismissing or interacting with the nudge
- Remains visible in the conversation thread as scrollable context
- Does not re-trigger if dismissed or if the user continues chatting
- Tapping "Sign in" CTA opens the Auth Sheet (Screen 2)
- No explicit dismiss button; nudge persists in history as contextual information

**Accessibility:**
- Entire nudge wrapped in `<article role="region" aria-label="Sign-in reminder">`
- Heading reads as h3
- Screen reader announces: "Region: Sign-in reminder. Your conversation won't be saved. Sign in or sign up to keep your chat history for later. Button: Sign in."
- CTA button read as: "Button: Sign in"

**Interactions & Animations:**
- Entry: no animation (renders in-place as part of conversation stream)
- On button click: Auth Sheet opens with 0.35s fade-in + scale

## User Flows

### Flow: US-1 — New user sign-up
Chat Home (logged-out, default) → [taps "Sign in" text or in-conversation nudge CTA] → Auth Sheet (default) → [completes Google or Apple SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation preserved)

### Flow: US-2 — Returning user sign-in
Chat Home (logged-out, default) → [taps "Sign in" text] → Auth Sheet (default) → [completes Google or Apple SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation preserved)

### Flow: US-3 — Explore before sign-up
Chat Home (logged-out, default) → [interacts with chat, sends first message] → Chat Home (conversation-active, logged-out) → [receives first AI response] → In-Conversation Sign-In Nudge displays → [continues chatting without signing in]

### Flow: US-4 — Persistent logged-out indicator
Chat Home (logged-out) — "Not signed in" text label persistently visible in top-right throughout all logged-out states. Ambient only, never blocking interaction.

### Flow: US-5 — Session expiry warning and conversation recovery
Chat Home (conversation-active, logged-out) → [10 minutes remaining on session TTL] → Session Expiry Warning Modal displays → [user taps "Sign up" or "Sign in"] → Auth Sheet (loading) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in, conversation preserved)

Alt: user dismisses modal → continues chatting → session expires → conversation lost, no further prompts.

### Flow: US-6 — Navigate-away protection
Chat Home (conversation-active, logged-out) → [user attempts back, close tab, or navigate away] → Navigate-Away Warning Modal displays → [user taps "Stay"] → returns to Chat Home (conversation-active)

Alt: user taps "Leave anyway" → navigation proceeds, conversation lost.

### Flow: US-7 — Conversation preserved on sign-up from logged-out session
Auth Sheet (opened from any CTA) → [completes SSO] → Chat Home (logged-in) → conversation history from logged-out session accessible in conversation history, visible immediately on home screen

## Accessibility

**Color & Contrast**
- Text primary (#F8F8F7) on background (#0A0A0F): contrast ratio 14.8:1 (WCAG AAA)
- Text primary on surface (#13131A): contrast ratio 13.6:1 (WCAG AAA)
- CTA text (white) on accent gradient: maintains WCAG AA minimum across full gradient range
- Error text (#e06c75) on background: contrast ratio 4.8:1 (WCAG AA)
- Glow treatment: decorative only, carries no meaning. Screen readers unaffected.
- Color is never the only signal for state change. Every state change accompanied by label, icon, or structural change.

**Interactive Elements**
- Logged-out indicator: non-interactive text, informational only
- Logged-in account name: clickable text with 44×44pt minimum touch target. Screen reader label: "Account menu trigger. [User name]. Activate to open account menu."
- "Sign in" CTAs: 44×44pt minimum touch targets across all screens
- Session Expiry and Navigate-Away modals: both CTA buttons 44×44pt minimum
- In-Conversation Nudge: "Sign in" button 44×44pt minimum
- All buttons have visible focus states (outline or background highlight)

**Modals & Sheets**
- Auth Sheet, Session Expiry Warning, and Navigate-Away modal all trap focus while open
- Escape key closes Auth Sheet and Session Expiry Warning (dismissible)
- Escape key does NOT close Navigate-Away modal (user must choose)
- Focus returned to trigger element after sheet/modal closes

**Keyboard Navigation (Web)**
- All interactive elements reachable by Tab
- Tab order: top-left wordmark (non-interactive) → top-right account indicator (clickable) → prompt bar input → Auth Sheet (if open, cycles through close → buttons → close)
- Modal/sheet focus loops prevent accidental escape
- No keyboard traps

**Screen Readers**
- Page title reads: "Health360 — Chat with your AI health coach"
- Wordmark: ARIA role `presentation` (duplicates page title)
- Tagline reads as static text
- In-conversation nudge: Screen reader reads "Region: Sign-in reminder. Your conversation won't be saved. Sign in or sign up to keep your chat history for later. Button: Sign in."
- Session Expiry Warning: "Dialog: Your conversation will expire in 10 minutes. Sign up or sign in to save it."
- Navigate-Away Warning: "Dialog: Your conversation will be lost. If you leave, your conversation won't be saved unless you sign in first."
- All CTAs include full context before button label

**RTL Support**
- Layout mirrors for RTL languages (Arabic, Hebrew)
- Logo moves to right, account indicator to left
- Wordmark and tagline remain centered
- Starter chips scroll direction reverses
- Accent gradient direction reverses (135° becomes 315°)
- All text alignment and spacing mirrors appropriately
- In-conversation nudge: "Sign in" button aligns left (mobile) or right (web) in RTL

**Mobile & Touch**
- One-thumb reachability: all primary actions in bottom third of screen on mobile
- Bottom sheets for all modals on mobile (native interaction model)
- Minimum 44×44pt touch targets, no exceptions

**Motion**
- Glow animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion`: if enabled, glow opacity is static at 75% (middle of cycle) with no pulsing, 80px blur maintained
- All transitions respect user motion preferences
- Loading spinners pause when `prefers-reduced-motion` is enabled

## Design System Updates

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Color Palette]

**Color Palette (Archon Labs, extracted from getarchon.dev)**

```
--bg:       #0A0A0F   // Page background — deep charcoal, near black
--surface:  #13131A   // Card/sheet surfaces — fractionally lighter
--text:     #F8F8F7   // Primary text — off-white, full contrast on dark bg
--muted:    rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.45)   // Secondary text, labels
--subtle:   rgba(248, 248, 247, 0.18)   // Tertiary text, placeholders
--border:   rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07)   // Borders and dividers

--violet:   #7C6FCD   // Accent — soft violet
--teal:     #4FAFA8   // Accent — muted teal

--error:    #e06c75
--warning:  #e5c07b
```

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Accent Gradient]

**Accent Gradient**

```css
linear-gradient(135deg, #7C6FCD, #4FAFA8)
```

Applied to: primary headings, logo/wordmark, signature UI moments, primary CTA backgrounds. Never in conversational content.

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Glow Animation]

**Glow (Signature Effect)**

Two independent radial glows — violet and teal — rendered separately and animated asynchronously.

**Violet glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.42) 0%, rgba(124, 111, 205, 0.14) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-violet {
  0%   { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 1.00; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.90; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.58; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-violet 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
```

**Teal glow:**
```css
background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.34) 0%, rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.11) 50%, transparent 100%);
filter: blur(80px);

@keyframes heartbeat-teal {
  0%   { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
  12%  { opacity: 0.95; transform: scale(1.20); }
  36%  { opacity: 0.85; transform: scale(1.13); }
  60%  { opacity: 0.55; transform: scale(0.98); }
  100% { opacity: 0.50; transform: scale(0.97); }
}
animation: heartbeat-teal 4s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) infinite;
animation-delay: -1.8s;   /* offset so violet and teal pulse out of sync */
```

**Usage rules:**
- Applied in exactly two places: (1) chat home background behind prompt bar, (2) Auth Sheet behind SSO buttons
- Positioned as a faint radial halo behind rounded pill containers and interactive elements
- Nowhere else in the product
- Each instance is independent — home glow and Auth Sheet glow never synchronize
- Both glows are decorative only (aria-hidden="true") and carry no semantic meaning
- Opacity cycle: 50–100% over 4s duration, cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1)
- Blur radius: 80px
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`: static opacity at 75% (middle of cycle), no pulsing, 80px blur maintained

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Typography]

**Typography**

- Font family: system-ui (no custom fonts required)
- Weights: 400 (regular), 500 (medium), 600 (semibold)
- Scaling: no custom font loading, system fonts only
- Gradient text (violet → teal): applied to primary headings, wordmark, primary CTA backgrounds, and UI chrome only — never in conversational content
- Body typography: clean, readable, system fonts with generous line-height

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Spacing & Shape]

**Spacing and Shape**

- Border radius: 12px (cards, inline alerts), 16px (sheets, modals), 24px (large containers), 40–48px (rounded pills/interactive containers), 9999px (minimal edge cases)
- Spacing scale: 8pt grid
- Negative space: 60–70% empty on hero screens, 30–40% empty within containers — signature Archon Labs aesthetic

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Transitions]

**Transitions**

- Fast: 0.15s ease
- Default: 0.20s ease
- Slow: 0.35s ease
- Sheet/Modal entry (mobile): 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) slide-up
- Sheet/Modal exit (mobile): 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) slide-down
- Modal entry (web): 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) fade-in + scale (0.95 → 1.0)
- Modal exit (web): 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.6, 1) fade-out + scale (1.0 → 0.95)

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

[PROPOSED ADDITION TO DESIGN_SYSTEM.md — Interactive Element Styling]

**Rounded Pill Containers (Prompt Bar, Auth Buttons, CTA Buttons)**

```css
border-radius: 40–48px;
border: 1px solid rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.15);   /* teal at 15% opacity */
background: rgba(19, 19, 26, 0.8);
padding: 14–16px;
transition: all 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  background: rgba(19, 19, 26, 1);
}

&:focus-within {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  background: rgba(19, 19, 26, 1);
}
```

**Primary CTA (Gradient Background)**

```css
background: linear-gradient(135deg, #7C6FCD, #4FAFA8);
border: none;
color: #F8F8F7;
border-radius: 40–48px;
padding: 14–16px;
transition: opacity 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  opacity: 0.9;
}

&:active {
  opacity: 0.85;
}
```

**Secondary CTA (Teal Outline)**

```css
border: 1px solid rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.15);
background: transparent;
color: #F8F8F7;
border-radius: 40–48px;
padding: 14–16px;
transition: all 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  background: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.05);
}

&:focus {
  border-color: rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.30);
  outline: 2px solid rgba(79, 175, 168, 0.5);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

**Destructive CTA (Error Outline)**

```css
border: 1px solid #e06c75;
background: transparent;
color: #e06c75;
border-radius: 40–48px;
padding: 14–16px;
transition: all 0.20s ease;

&:hover {
  background: rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.05);
}

&:focus {
  border-color: #e06c75;
  outline: 2px solid rgba(224, 108, 117, 0.5);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

Glow effect positioned behind: radial halo, 50–100% opacity, 80px blur, independent animation per instance (offset -1.8s on teal).

[END PROPOSED ADDITION]

## Open Questions
- [type: engineering] [blocking: no] Unauthenticated session TTL is provisionally set at 60 minutes inactivity-based expiry with a warning at 10 minutes remaining — needs infrastructure confirmation before engineering begins.
onboarding.productSpec:
# Onboarding — Product Spec

## Problem
New and returning users need a frictionless path from first landing to the main chat home screen. Unauthenticated users risk losing their conversation history without realizing it, which undermines trust and conversion. The onboarding experience must get users to the home screen quickly, make the value of signing in clear without forcing it, and ensure no one is surprised by data loss.

## Target Users
- **New users** — arriving for the first time, no existing account
- **Returning users** — have an existing account, signing back in
- **Logged-out new users** — new users who choose to explore before signing up

## User Stories
1. As a new user, I want to sign up quickly so that I can start using Health360 without unnecessary friction.
2. As a returning user, I want to sign in and land directly on the home screen so that I can pick up where I left off.
3. As a logged-out user, I want to explore the product before committing to sign up so that I can understand its value first.
4. As a logged-out user, I want a subtle, persistent indicator that I am not signed in so that I am never surprised by the implications of being logged out.
5. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned before my session expires so that I can sign up and save my conversation before it's lost.
6. As a logged-out user, I want to be warned when I try to navigate away or close the tab so that I don't accidentally lose my conversation.
7. As a logged-out user, I want to know that signing in will preserve my conversation so that I have a clear reason to create an account at that moment.
8. As a new user completing sign-up from a logged-out session, I want my existing conversation to carry over so that I don't lose the context I've already built.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Unauthenticated users can access the chat interface and interact with the AI without signing in.
2. A persistent, non-dismissible indicator is visible to logged-out users in the top-right area of the screen, clearly stating they are not signed in.
3. The indicator does not block interaction or prompt sign-up — it is ambient awareness only.
4. The logged-out indicator remains visible at all times while the user is not authenticated.
5. New users can sign up via SSO (Google, Apple Sign-In) only — no password-based registration.
6. Returning users can sign in via SSO and are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
7. New users who complete sign-up are taken directly to the main chat home screen upon successful authentication.
8. When a logged-out user's session is approaching expiry (at 10 minutes remaining) AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a warning is surfaced informing them their conversation will be lost if they do not sign in.
9. When a logged-out user attempts to navigate away or close the tab AND they have at least one conversation exchange, a custom in-app modal is surfaced informing them their conversation will be lost.
10. After the first complete AI response in a session, an in-conversation nudge surfaces informing the logged-out user that their conversation will not be saved unless they sign in, rendered as a conversational system message with a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action.
11. The in-conversation nudge fires exactly once per session and does not repeat on subsequent exchanges.
12. Both warnings and the in-conversation nudge include a clear call-to-action to sign up or sign in.
13. Upon completing sign-up or sign-in from any warning CTA, the user's conversation is fully preserved and accessible in their conversation history.
14. If the logged-out user has no conversation history, no session expiry or navigation warning is shown.
15. If the user dismisses a warning and does not sign in, the session expires and the conversation is lost — no further prompts.
16. There is no forced sign-up wall at any point in the logged-out experience.
17. The path from landing page to authenticated home screen requires a maximum of 3 user interactions: (1) landing on the logged-out chat interface, (2) tapping a sign-in or sign-up call-to-action, (3) completing SSO and being taken directly to the authenticated home screen. No intermediate profile setup, goal-setting, or confirmation screens are permitted.
18. Goal setting does not occur during onboarding — it happens post-onboarding through the AI conversation.
19. On initial page load, the UI defaults to logged-out mode immediately while authentication state resolution occurs in the background.
20. If authentication state resolution succeeds within 2 seconds of initial load, the user transitions to authenticated state without visual disruption and conversation history loads.
21. If authentication state resolution fails or exceeds 2 seconds, the user remains in logged-out mode and a retry affordance appears only upon confirmed auth failure.

## Edge Cases
- User signs up from the warning CTA but the session expires before authentication completes — conversation cannot be recovered; surface a clear, friendly message.
- User begins sign-up flow from the warning but abandons it — session continues to tick down; conversation is lost on expiry with no additional prompt.
- User dismisses the navigate-away modal but remains on the page — modal does not re-trigger unless they attempt to navigate away again.
- User has multiple tabs open with separate logged-out conversations — each tab is treated independently.
- Returning user attempts to sign in but SSO provider is unavailable — surface a clear error; do not block the logged-out experience.
- New user completes SSO but the account creation step fails — handle gracefully; do not drop the user into an inconsistent state.
- Session expiry warning fires at 10 minutes remaining — sufficient lead time to complete SSO sign-up before expiry.
- The in-conversation nudge fires once per session — it does not repeat on every exchange.
- Authentication state resolution fails on initial page load — user remains in logged-out mode and may attempt retry; retry attempt should not loop indefinitely or create cascading failures.
- Authentication state resolution exceeds 2 seconds but succeeds before user initiates action — user receives transparent transition to authenticated state without losing any interaction context.

## Non-Goals
- Goal setting, profile building, and AI coaching — these begin after the user reaches the home screen.
- Password-based authentication — SSO only.
- Forced sign-up walls or conversion prompts outside of the defined warning triggers and in-conversation nudge.
- Auto-saving or server-side backup of logged-out conversations without explicit user action.
- Changing session expiry duration — infrastructure concern outside this spec.
- Social or referral flows — not part of the initial vision.

## Design Direction
**Dark mode primary — Archon Labs aesthetic (getarchon.dev).** Visual language: minimal, high negative space. The interface recedes; the conversation leads. Chrome is nearly invisible until needed.

**Color palette (extracted from getarchon.dev):**
- Background: `#0A0E27` (dark navy, almost black)
- Surfaces: `#151B38` (fractionally lighter, subtle elevation)
- Text primary: `#F5F5F5` (off-white, full contrast on dark background)
- Text muted: `rgba(245, 245, 245, 0.6)` (neutral grey for secondary labels)
- Accent gradient: 135° from `#8B7FE8` (soft violet) to `#4FADA8` (muted teal)

**Signature treatment: Pulsing glow effect**
- Radial gradient: violet-teal (same palette as accent gradient)
- Animation: async heartbeat pulse (not synchronized to user input or other glow instances)
- Opacity cycle: 25% → 35% → 25% over 2.5 seconds, ease-in-out cubic bezier (cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1))
- Position: fixed behind UI elements (prompt bar and Auth Sheet buttons)
- Applied in exactly two places: (1) chat home background behind prompt bar, (2) Auth Sheet behind SSO buttons
- Behavior as conversation fills: blur radius shrinks (200px → 100px), opacity reduces (25–35% → 12–16%) over ~4 messages or 60% viewport fill
- Minimum state: 12–16% opacity, ~100px blur radius — still present, clearly visible
- Each glow instance pulses independently — no synchronization between home glow and Auth Sheet glow
- Nowhere else in the product
- Conveys: ambient warmth, alive-ness without distraction

## Open Questions
- [type: engineering] [blocking: no] Unauthenticated session TTL is provisionally set at 60 minutes inactivity-based expiry with a warning at 10 minutes remaining — needs infrastructure confirmation before engineering begins.
